originalTitle: Valve says iFixit will keep selling Steam Deck batteries after all
summary: Valve has been incredibly friendly to customers who need repairs - which is why it was so surprising to hear that Valve was already discontinuing the battery for the Steam Deck LCD handheld. It would have meant you could no longer just buy a battery from iFixit and install it yourself. Why would Valve stop […]
content: Valve has been incredibly friendly to customers who need repairs — which is why it was so surprising to hear that Valve was already discontinuing the battery for the Steam Deck LCD handheld. It would have meant you could no longer just buy a battery from iFixit and install it yourself. Why would Valve stop stocking the most important replacement part just as people are starting to need it? Here is Valve’s answer to The Verge, as of 5PM ET: “We just confirmed with iFixit that they plan to have batteries back in stock by next week.” “iFixit will be getting the same OEM parts sourced through Valve’s partners that they always have,” spokesperson Kaci Aitchison Boyle tells me. The Reddit story wasn’t necessarily false. Earlier today, iFixit CEO Kyle Wiens confirmed to The Verge that it had indeed heard that Valve would no longer make replacement batteries or screens for the original Steam Deck LCD. But by afternoon, Valve and iFixit had already managed to change that. “They have hooked us up with a supplier, we’re working on it,” Wiens now tells me. And if Valve does decide to sunset the part in the future, iFixit says it’ll be ready to take up the torch by using an aftermarket supplier instead. “I want people to know we are going to find a way to get batteries for these things,” says Wiens. [Image: iFixit staffers said the other day that Valve was sunsetting the parts. That’s no longer true of the battery, at least not right now. https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/chrome_AXwjez6Ra8.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] Wiens says Valve has “been a really great partner.” He suspects that Valve and its suppliers simply bet wrong on how many replacement parts they needed to produce. “If you get the forecast wrong, you run out, or you go wrong in the other direction and spend way too much money on parts sitting around doing nobody any good.” If Valve did discontinue this battery, it likely wouldn’t have anything to do with the EU regulations on user-replacable batteries. Starting in February 2027, new EU laws will require companies to make batteries user-replaceable in the devices they sell. Those regulations have already prompted Nintendo to discontinue the original Switch in Europe, and create a new EU-only version of the Switch 2 with a replaceable battery. But that shouldn’t affect the Steam Deck LCD, because Valve stopped selling it last December. And I’m not sure US laws would necessarily keep the battery alive, either. Some states have right-to-repair laws that force companies to offer parts for a certain number of years, but most explicitly exclude game consoles. (Would Valve want to argue it’s a PC instead?) Regardless of how easy it is to get this battery, it’s not the easiest to replace, as iFixit explained in 2022 when it first started stocking official Steam Deck components with Valve’s help. It’s strongly glued into the frame, and you can’t just rip it out without potentially causing a fire; you have to carefully loosen the glue. In a 2022 interview with The Verge, Valve designers told me why they used glue, but admitted they weren’t satisfied with that adhesive. I keep hoping we’ll see electrically removable adhesive in handhelds; iFixit’s CEO tells me shock-to-release glues are “often too expensive to include in the original device,” but are possible aftermarket if you’re willing to spend “a couple bucks extra.” Perhaps by the time you need an aftermarket battery from a different supplier, it’ll be even better than the original.
chineseTitle: Valve 表示 iFixit 将继续销售 Steam Deck 电池
chineseSummary: Valve 对需要维修的客户非常友好 - 这就是为什么当听到 Valve 已经停止为 Steam Deck LCD 手持设备提供电池时，我们感到非常惊讶。这意味着您不能再从 iFixit 购买电池并自行安装。为什么阀门会停止[...]
chineseContent: Valve 对需要维修的客户非常友好，这就是为什么当听到 Valve 已经停止为 Steam Deck LCD 手持设备提供电池时，我们感到非常惊讶。这意味着您不能再从 iFixit 购买电池并自行安装。为什么 Valve 会在人们开始需要最重要的替换零件时停止储备它？截至美国东部时间下午 5 点，Valve 对 The Verge 的答复如下：“我们刚刚与 iFixit 确认，他们计划在下周之前恢复电池库存。” “iFixit 将一如既往地通过 Valve 的合作伙伴获得相同的 OEM 零件，”发言人 Kaci Aitchison Boyle 告诉我。 Reddit 上的故事并不一定是假的。今天早些时候，iFixit 首席执行官凯尔·维恩斯 (Kyle Wiens) 向 The Verge 证实，确实听说 Valve 将不再为原始 Steam Deck LCD 生产替换电池或屏幕。但到了下午，Valve 和 iFixit 已经设法改变了这一状况。 “他们已经为我们联系了一家供应商，我们正在努力，”维恩斯现在告诉我。如果 Valve 决定在未来取消该部件，iFixit 表示将准备通过售后供应商来接手这个部件。 “我想让人们知道我们将找到一种方法来为这些东西获取电池，”维恩斯说。 [图片：iFixit 员工前几天表示 Valve 正在淘汰这些零件。对于电池来说，情况已不再如此，至少现在不是。 https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/chrome_AXwjez6Ra8.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] Wiens 表示 Valve “是一个非常出色的合作伙伴”。他怀疑 Valve 及其供应商只是在需要生产多少替换零件上打错了赌注。 “如果你的预测错误，你就会用完，或者你在另一个方向出错
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
